standard.part1: Direct answer, reason, concrete detail, natural close.
standard.part2: Cue-card answer with clear context, required details, development, feeling, and reflection.
standard.part3: Discussion answer with opinion, reason, example or extension, contrast, and balanced close.
standard.part2_combo: Adaptable story tailored to the real cue card, not a fixed universal story.

topics.title: 新法律 + 重要决定 + 动物故事书 + 困难后成功的人

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’m going to talk about a time I made a very important decision that was directly influenced by a new law in my country, and I’ll connect it to a children’s book about animals that helped me understand the situation better. A couple of years ago, the government introduced a new law about mandatory microchipping for all pet dogs. At first, I thought, “Oh, that’s just another bureaucratic thing.” But then my neighbour’s dog got lost during a thunderstorm, and they couldn’t find him for three weeks. That really shook me. I had a golden retriever named Max at the time, and I realised that if he ever got lost, I’d be completely helpless. So I made a very deliberate decision: I took Max to the vet, paid for the microchip, and registered him in the national database. It wasn’t expensive, but it felt like a big responsibility. Around that same period, I read a children’s book called *The Lost Puppy* to my little cousin. It’s a simple story about a puppy who wanders too far from home and can’t find his way back because he has no collar or tag. A kind old lady finds him, feeds him, and eventually helps reunite him with his family. The book has a very gentle message about safety and belonging. Honestly, that story made the new law feel much more human to me. It wasn’t just a regulation; it was about protecting something you love. So that decision—to chip Max—felt like a natural response to both the law and that little story. It turned an abstract rule into a personal commitment, and I still feel good about it every time I see Max sleeping on the sofa.

topics.title: 无聊地方 + 科技问题 + 近期看的节目 + 花很久回信息

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: When I was in my early twenties, I spent a summer working in a small, dusty town in the middle of nowhere. It was incredibly boring — nothing but one main street, a few shops that closed at five, and endless hot afternoons. I remember going to the local library just to sit in the air conditioning, even though I’d already read every magazine in the place. The lack of anything to do was almost suffocating. To pass the time, I started watching this documentary series about space exploration. It was honestly fascinating — I’d never really understood how rockets worked before, but the animations and explanations were so clear. One episode showed the challenges of communication delays between Earth and Mars, and it reminded me of a weird tech problem I’d been having with my phone. Messages would sometimes arrive hours late, or not at all, which made me wonder if something was wrong with the network or just my device. I spent ages trying to fix it, even resetting the phone twice. That also explains why I take so long to reply to messages sometimes. People think I’m ignoring them, but really, my phone just decides to deliver everything in one big batch. It’s frustrating, but at least I learned something about space while waiting for texts to come through.

topics.title: 高建筑 + 特别场合食物 + 喜欢拜访但不想住的家 + 改变重要想法

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’d like to talk about a time I visited a really tall building – the Shanghai Tower. I went there last year with a couple of friends during a trip to China. The building itself is over 600 metres high, and honestly, when you stand at the bottom and look up, it’s almost dizzying. We took the elevator to the observation deck on the 118th floor. The ride was incredibly fast but smooth, and my ears popped a bit. Once we got up there, the view of the whole city was absolutely breathtaking – all those skyscrapers looked like tiny toys from that height. I remember feeling a little wobbly on my legs, especially when I stepped onto the glass floor section. That was both thrilling and terrifying at the same time. Afterwards, we went to a restaurant in the building that served a special local dish called xiaolongbao – those soup dumplings. They were served in a fancy bamboo steamer and tasted so fresh and delicate. It was the perfect way to end the visit. As for why I’d like to visit but not live there – well, living in a super tall building sounds exciting, but I think I’d miss having a garden or even just a balcony where I can feel the breeze directly. Also, waiting for the elevator every time I want to go out would drive me crazy. So, I’d rather just visit for the view and the food.

topics.title: 喜欢种菜的人 + 团队中工作 + 名人广告 + 坏掉的电子设备

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’m going to talk about my neighbour, Uncle Chen, who is absolutely obsessed with growing vegetables. He’s in his late sixties, retired, and spends nearly every morning in his small backyard garden. He grows tomatoes, chillies, spring onions, and even bitter gourd, which I personally can’t stand, but he’s very proud of them. What really strikes me is how he treats gardening like a serious team project. His wife helps with watering, his grandson collects earthworms for compost, and sometimes I get roped in to carry heavy pots. So in a way, it’s teamwork, though not in an office setting. He often jokes that without his ‘team’, the vegetables would be a disaster. Another thing about Uncle Chen is that he once bought a brand of fertiliser just because a famous TV gardener endorsed it. He showed me the advertisement on his phone and said, ‘If this guy uses it, it must be good.’ I found it amusing because he’s usually quite sceptical about ads. One day, his old electric water pump broke down. It made a terrible grinding noise and then just stopped. He was so upset because without it, watering the whole garden by hand would take hours. I helped him find a replacement part online, and he fixed it himself. Seeing him repair that device reminded me how resourceful and determined he is when it comes to his beloved plants.

topics.title: 最喜欢的城市 + 想旅游的地方 + 喜欢现场体育赛事 + 推荐旅行过的地方

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’d like to talk about Barcelona, which is not only my favourite city but also a place I’d strongly recommend for travel. I visited it two years ago during summer, and it completely changed how I think about cities. What makes Barcelona so special is the blend of culture, architecture, and energy. Walking through the Gothic Quarter, you’re surrounded by narrow medieval streets, but then suddenly you turn a corner and see something like the Sagrada Familia—this massive, unfinished basilica by Gaudí. It’s breathtaking in a way photos can’t capture. I also loved the local markets, especially La Boqueria, where I tried jamón ibérico and fresh seafood. The vibe there is loud, colourful, and very authentic. Now, as someone who enjoys live sports, I also attended a football match at Camp Nou. That was an unforgettable experience—the crowd singing, the drums, the sheer intensity when Barcelona scored. Even if you’re not a huge fan, the atmosphere alone is electric. So if you’re looking for a travel recommendation, I’d say Barcelona offers everything: history, food, art, and live sports. It’s a city that feels alive every single moment. Just be prepared for crowds in summer, and book tickets for the big attractions in advance. Honestly, I’d go back in a heartbeat.

topics.title: 发小 + 早起经历 + 近期改变的计划 + 别人帮助解决问题 + 长久目标

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’ve known my childhood friend, Li Wei, since we were five years old—our mothers were colleagues, so we basically grew up together. Last month, I had an early morning experience that completely changed my approach to planning. I’d been struggling with a big problem at work: I couldn’t figure out how to reorganise our team’s chaotic project schedule. One Saturday, Li Wei dragged me out for a 6 a.m. run in the park, even though I’m not a morning person. We were jogging past the lake when I started complaining about the schedule mess. He just laughed and said, “You always try to fix everything at once. Why not break it into tiny steps, like this run—one kilometre at a time?” That simple advice clicked instantly. Since then, I’ve made a concrete change: I now wake up at 5:30 every weekday to plan my day in 30-minute blocks, tackling just three priority tasks. It’s been only three weeks, but my productivity has doubled. Looking ahead, my long-term goal is to become a project manager who can lead international teams. This early-morning habit and Li Wei’s practical advice have shown me that small, consistent actions—not grand schemes—are what truly build momentum toward big ambitions.

topics.title: 童年玩具/生活中离不开的东西

answers.question: 童年玩具 Describe the toy you played with as a child You should say: What it was What it was like How you played with it And explain why you played with it 生活中离不开的东西 Describe something that you can't live without (not a computer/phone) You should say: What it is What you do with it How it helps you in your life And explain why you can't live without it
answers.answer: I’d like to talk about a toy I played with a lot as a child—it was a simple wooden spinning top. It wasn’t anything fancy, just a cone-shaped piece of wood painted in bright red and yellow stripes, with a metal tip at the bottom. To make it spin, you’d wind a string tightly around it and then throw it onto the ground with a quick flick of your wrist. I used to play with it in the courtyard with my cousins. We’d take turns spinning our tops and try to knock each other’s over, or see whose top could spin the longest. There was a satisfying *whirring* sound as it spun, and the colours blurred together. I think I loved it because it was so simple yet required a bit of skill—if you didn’t wind the string right, it would just flop over. It also gave me a sense of control, and it was something I could do outdoors without needing batteries or screens. Looking back, that top taught me patience and coordination. It’s funny how a small wooden toy could bring so much joy, but it really did. I’d say it’s something I couldn’t live without back then—not in a dramatic way, but because it was my escape from boredom and a way to connect with others. Even now, I keep it on a shelf as a reminder of those carefree days.

topics.title: 远距离步行/想共度时光的人/计划多人活动/照片/美丽天空/户外运动的地方

answers.question: 远距离步行/想共度时光的人/计划多人活动/照片/美丽天空/户外运动的地方 Describe an outdoor sport you would like to do Describe a photo that you enjoy looking at Describe a time when you and some other people made a plan to do an activity Describe a beautiful sky you enjoyed seeing Describe a place you went to and an outdoor activity you did there
answers.answer: I’d like to talk about a time a few friends and I planned a long-distance walk along the coastal path near Brighton, which also gave me one of the most beautiful skies I’ve ever seen, and a photo I still enjoy looking at. We had been talking for weeks about doing something outdoors together, and eventually we settled on a 15-kilometre walk from Brighton to a village called Rottingdean. The plan was simple—meet early, bring snacks, and take it at our own pace. When the day came, the sky was incredible. It was early autumn, and the sunrise turned the whole horizon into layers of orange, pink, and soft grey, with the sea reflecting the colours. I took a photo just as we stopped for a break on a cliff edge, and in the picture you can see my friend laughing with the wind in her hair and that sky behind us. That walk was more than just exercise—it was the kind of outdoor sport I’d happily do again. We didn’t rush, we talked about everything, and even when it started drizzling, nobody complained. The photo still makes me smile because it captures that easy, shared happiness. So for me, that walk combined the place, the sky, the activity, and the people perfectly.

topics.title: 给别人建议 + 擅长做计划的人 + 乐于助人的人+ 鼓励保护自然的人

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’d like to talk about my uncle, Mr. Chen, who is someone I truly admire. He’s in his early fifties, works as a landscape architect, and has this quiet but firm way of encouraging people to protect nature. I remember last spring, I was helping him plant trees in a small park near his home. He didn’t just hand me a shovel and say “dig a hole.” Instead, he explained why native species matter—how their roots hold the soil, how they need less water, and how they support local birds and insects. He’s also incredibly good at planning. Before we started, he had sketched out exactly where each tree should go, based on sunlight and drainage. That day, he even gave me advice on how to plan my own weekend gardening. He said, “Start small. Observe your space for a week. Then make a list.” It sounds simple, but his approach really helped me avoid wasting time and money. What strikes me most is his willingness to help. He once spent an entire Saturday teaching a group of schoolchildren how to build bird feeders from recycled materials. He didn’t charge anything; he just wanted them to feel connected to nature. For me, he’s a perfect example of someone who combines planning, generosity, and a genuine love for the environment.

topics.title: 自行车/摩托车/汽车旅行 + 外国的短期停留 + 想见的名人 + 花费超过预期的物品

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’d like to describe a time I travelled by motorbike in a foreign country for a short stay. A couple of years ago, I went to Vietnam for a week, and I decided to rent a motorbike to explore the countryside around Hoi An. I’m not an experienced rider, so it was quite an adventure. The roads were dusty and busy with scooters, but the scenery was stunning—rice paddies, small villages, and locals selling fruit by the roadside. I stopped at a tiny café for iced coffee, and the owner, an elderly woman, showed me photos of her grandchildren. That moment felt so genuine. Now, if I could meet a famous person during that trip, I’d choose Anthony Bourdain. He had such a deep connection with Vietnam—he ate street food, talked to locals, and understood the culture. I’d love to ask him about his favourite hidden spots in Hoi An. As for what cost more than I expected, it was the motorbike rental itself. The deposit was high, and I had to pay extra for insurance because I wasn’t used to the traffic. I ended up spending nearly double what I’d planned. Still, the freedom of riding through those winding roads was absolutely worth it.

topics.title: 喜欢画画的孩子 + 最近读过的故事 + 有趣的建筑 + 不享受的音乐活动

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’d like to describe a time when I read a story about a child who loved drawing. The story was called *The Dot*, written by Peter Reynolds, and I came across it while browsing in a bookshop last year. The main character was a little girl named Vashti, who thought she couldn’t draw at all. Her teacher simply encouraged her to make a mark and see where it went. So Vashti jabbed at her paper with a marker, making a single dot. The teacher framed it, and that small act of recognition sparked something in her. Vashti began experimenting with dots of different colours, sizes, and patterns, eventually creating an entire exhibition of dot paintings. What struck me most was how her confidence grew from such a tiny starting point. The story reminded me of my own cousin, who used to draw endlessly as a child. He’d cover every scrap of paper with dinosaurs and spaceships, and our grandmother kept a special drawer just for his artwork. Reading about Vashti brought back that warmth – how creativity doesn’t need to be perfect, it just needs encouragement. I found the story emotionally touching because it showed how a single supportive comment can transform a child’s self-belief. It’s a simple but powerful lesson that stayed with me long after I closed the book.

topics.title: 朋友学的技能 + App/程序 + 机智解决问题的人 + 鼓励别人做不愿做的事

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’d like to talk about my friend Kevin, who learned a pretty impressive skill last year—mobile app development. He wasn’t a tech guy at all; he worked in marketing. But he got really frustrated with how complicated it was to split bills during group trips, so he decided to build his own app to solve that problem. The app he created is called “Split Ease.” It’s super simple: you enter who paid for what, and it automatically calculates who owes whom, even accounting for tips and taxes. We all use it now. Honestly, it saved our friendship during a chaotic trip to Thailand. One guy kept forgetting what he spent, but the app tracked everything, so no arguments. What really stands out is how Kevin solved a problem we all faced. Instead of just complaining, he actually learned coding from YouTube tutorials and built a working solution. That’s pretty clever, right? He even added a feature where you can settle debts with a single tap—so simple, yet genius. Also, Kevin encouraged me to learn something new when I was too lazy. I hated cooking, but he challenged me to make one dish a week using his app’s recipe section. He kept checking in, and now I actually enjoy it. So yeah, he’s someone who pushes you to do things you’d rather avoid, but in a supportive way.

topics.title: 商场 + 禁用手机的场合 + 微笑的场合 + 近期观影

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’m going to talk about a time when I went to a shopping mall and ended up in a situation where I had to put my phone away – and also where smiling felt completely natural. It happened just last month, when I went with my cousin to a big mall in the city centre. We were there to watch a new film that had just come out – an animated comedy about a family of penguins. The cinema was on the top floor of the mall, and we had about forty minutes before the screening started, so we decided to walk around. That’s when my cousin spotted a small pop-up exhibition called “The Quiet Corner.” It was a space designed to encourage people to disconnect from their devices for a while. There were signs everywhere saying “No phones allowed,” and visitors were asked to leave their phones in little lockers before entering. At first, I felt a bit anxious without my phone, but once inside, I noticed how peaceful it was. There were soft cushions, dim lighting, and paintings of nature on the walls. After a few minutes, I looked at my cousin and we both smiled – not because anything funny happened, but because we felt genuinely relaxed. It was a warm, shared smile that said, “This is actually nice.” Later, we went to watch the penguin film, and I smiled a lot during that too. But that quiet moment in the exhibition – smiling without a phone in my hand – felt more memorable. It reminded me that sometimes the best experiences happen when we simply put our devices down and connect with people face to face.

topics.title: 完美工作 + 想拥有的科技产品 + 擅长做计划的人 + 发挥想象力

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: Let me walk you through a single story that can flexibly answer all four prompts. **Cue Card: Describe a perfect job you would like to have** The perfect job for me would be designing sustainable housing for communities affected by natural disasters. I first imagined this role after a typhoon hit a coastal area near my hometown. I saw how many families lost everything, and I thought, “If I could design homes that are both affordable and resilient, that would be incredibly meaningful.” In this job, I would work with engineers, local governments, and families themselves, listening to their needs and then creating practical, eco-friendly housing solutions. I’d use recycled materials and solar energy systems, so the homes are not only safe but also self-sufficient. The best part would be seeing families move into a place they can call home again. What makes this job perfect for me is the combination of creativity and real-world impact. I wouldn’t just be drawing plans in an office; I’d be solving problems that affect people’s daily lives. That sense of purpose is what I value most. Honestly, I can’t imagine anything more fulfilling than using my skills to rebuild lives, one house at a time. --- **Adapted for: A piece of technology you would like to own** The technology I’d most like to own is a portable 3D printer that can use recycled plastic. With this device, I could quickly produce building components for emergency shelters. Imagine arriving at a disaster site and printing walls, roof panels, or even furniture right there, using waste plastic collected from the area. It would speed up reconstruction enormously and reduce costs. --- **Adapted for: Someone who is good at planning** My cousin is the best planner I know. She works as a project manager for a construction company, and when she coordinated a relief housing project, she created a timeline that accounted for weather, material delivery, and volunteer shifts. She even had backup plans for each phase. Her careful thinking taught me that good planning isn’t about rigidity; it’s about anticipating

topics.title: 让你骄傲的家人 + 对家庭重要的东西 + 鼓励你保护自然的人 + 乐于助人的人

answers.question: 同学可以结合题库的具体问题，练习素材。 1.为了更好的匹配每一道题，素材本身的字数会超过真实的考试同学们能表达字数。 2.针对不同的问法，同学的开头和结尾需要进行调整。 3.一定要注意表达的自然程度~必要时增加重音，表情和手势
answers.answer: I’m going to talk about my grandfather, who’s someone I’m really proud of and who also taught me a lot about helping others and protecting nature. He’s in his late seventies now, but he still spends hours every day in his vegetable garden. He grows everything organically—tomatoes, chillies, spinach, even some fruit trees. What makes me proud is that he doesn’t just do it for himself. Every week, he packs up baskets of fresh produce and delivers them to elderly neighbours who can’t get out much. He never charges them a cent. For him, sharing food is just part of being a decent human being. He also taught me to respect the environment. When I was a kid, he’d take me to the local river to fish, but he always made sure we picked up every bit of rubbish before we left. He’d say, “If you take from nature, you have to give back.” That stuck with me. Now I try to follow his example—I compost kitchen waste and avoid plastic packaging as much as I can. To me, my grandfather represents what really matters in a family: generosity, hard work, and a sense of responsibility toward others and the planet. He’s not rich or famous, but he’s made a real difference in our community. That’s why I’m so proud of him.

topics.title: 想见的名人/时尚人士/奇装异服的人/喜欢的歌手

answers.question: 想见的名人/奇装异服的人/喜欢的歌手 Describe a famous person you would like to meet. Describe a person who you think wears unusual clothes. Describe a singer you like
answers.answer: I’d love to meet the singer Billie Eilish. I’ve been a fan of her music for about three years now, ever since I heard her song *bad guy* on a playlist. What really draws me to her is not just her voice, but the way she presents herself. The first time I saw one of her music videos, I was honestly a bit shocked. She often wears extremely oversized, baggy clothes—sometimes neon green or black-and-white, with layers that make her look almost shapeless. It’s unusual because most pop stars wear flashy, revealing outfits, but she deliberately covers up. She’s said it’s to avoid being judged on her body, which I think is really powerful. Her style is like a statement: “I’m here for the music, not for your gaze.” If I ever met her, I’d want to ask about her songwriting process. I’ve read that she writes lyrics with her brother in a tiny bedroom studio. I’d also just thank her for being so honest in her songs about anxiety and growing up. I imagine she’d be a bit shy in person, but very direct. Meeting her would feel less like meeting a celebrity and more like talking to a creative friend who happens to be famous.

topics.title: 家庭重要设备/喜欢的电视剧

answers.question: Describe a piece of equipment that is important in your home You should say: What it is How often you use it With whom you use it And expDescribe a TV series you like You should say: What is the name of the TV series When you watched it What it is about And explain why you like it.lain why it is important；
answers.answer: I’d like to talk about a TV series I really like called *Breaking Bad*. I watched it about two years ago during my summer break. The series is about a high school chemistry teacher named Walter White, who finds out he has cancer. To secure his family’s financial future, he starts cooking and selling methamphetamine with a former student, Jesse Pinkman. The story follows his gradual transformation from a kind, law-abiding man into a ruthless drug lord. I usually watched it alone in my room on my laptop, often late at night because I couldn’t stop after one episode. The show is important to me because it’s not just entertaining—it makes you think about morality, choices, and consequences. The character development is incredible; you see Walter change step by step, and it feels real. The tension in each episode kept me completely hooked. I also liked how the show used symbols, like the colour of clothing, to show changes in the characters. It’s the kind of series that stays with you long after you finish it, and I often recommend it to friends.

topics.title: 有趣的建筑/和你分享东西的人

answers.question: Describe an interesting building You should say. Where it is What it looks like What function it has And explain why you think it is interesting； Describe someone who is willing to openly share things with you You should say: Who this person is How you knew this person What this person openly shares And how you feel about the sharing
answers.answer: **Describe an interesting building** I’d like to talk about the Lotus Temple in New Delhi, India. It’s a Baháʼí House of Worship shaped like a giant lotus flower, with 27 white marble petals arranged in clusters of three. The petals curve outward from a central dome, and the whole structure sits in a nine-sided pool of water, so it looks like it’s floating. Its function is primarily as a place for meditation and prayer – people of any religion can go inside to sit quietly or reflect. There are no idols or rituals, just a simple hall with benches. I find it interesting because the design isn’t just decorative – it symbolises purity and unity, which matches the Baháʼí belief in the oneness of humanity. Also, the way natural light filters through the petals creates a calm, almost ethereal atmosphere. I remember visiting on a hot afternoon, and the marble stayed cool, and the silence inside felt completely separate from the chaotic city outside. **Describe someone who is willing to openly share things with you** This person is my colleague, Ananya. I met her two years ago when we started working in the same marketing team. She openly shares her knowledge – for example, she taught me how to use advanced Excel formulas for data analysis, and she’s always the first to send useful articles or templates to the group chat. She also shares her mistakes, like when she accidentally sent a draft to a client – she told everyone what went wrong so we could avoid it. I feel grateful and a bit inspired. Her sharing isn’t about showing off; it’s practical and genuine. It makes our team more efficient and less stressful, because we don’t have to figure everything out alone. Honestly, it’s rare to find someone who gives away their hard-earned tips without expecting anything in return.
